chunk_summary: Guidelines for monitoring severe acute malnutrition during inpatient stabilization, including stop rules for IV fluids, rehydration, feeding, and failure-to-respond triggers within critical timepoints.
learning_objectives: Understand the stop rules for IV fluid administration and rehydration in SAM management.
Learn the critical timepoints for monitoring failure to respond in SAM care.
Identify diagnostic reassessment triggers for persistent oedema or lack of appetite recovery.
Track feeding intake, weight, and oedema resolution during inpatient care.
key_facts: Monitor pulse and respiratory rate every 5–10 minutes during IV fluid administration; stop infusion if pulse rises by 15/min or respiratory rate rises by 5/min.
Reassess hydration every 30 minutes for the first 2 hours and hourly thereafter during oral or NG rehydration; stop if tachypnea, rising pulse, or periorbital puffiness develops.
Daily weight tracking and oedema grading are essential until resolution.
Appetite recovery by day 4 is critical; failure triggers reassessment for infections, electrolyte issues, or comorbidities like TB or HIV.
Failure to gain ≥5 g/kg/day for three consecutive days after transition to free feeding requires structured diagnostic review.
reference_quote: For any child receiving IV fluids for shock, Indian ward thresholds operationalize safety: record pulse and respiratory rate at the start and at least every 5–10 minutes, and stop the infusion immediately if respiratory rate rises by 5/min or pulse rises by 15/min. (Reference 1)
document_key_topics: Stop rules for IV fluids and rehydration
Feeding and weight tracking in SAM care
